query: Can you recommend a paper that uses an NLI model for sentence-level relation extraction using hypothesis generation and verification with entity-type constraints?
criteria: NLI model sentence-level relation extraction hypothesis generation verification
sentence-level relation extraction natural language inference

title.value: Exploring Fine-grained Entity Type Constraints for Distantly Supervised Relation Extraction

authors_year.value: Yang Liu, Kang Liu, Liheng Xu, Jun Zhao (2014)

recommendation_reason.value: Exploring Fine-grained Entity Type Constraints for Distantly Supervised Relation Extraction

supported_research_question.value: Distantly supervised relation extraction, which can automatically generate training data by aligning facts in the existing knowledge bases to text, has gained much attention.

method.value: Distantly supervised relation extraction, which can automatically generate training data by aligning facts in the existing knowledge bases to text, has gained much attention.

data_or_scenario.value: Distantly supervised relation extraction, which can automatically generate training data by aligning facts in the existing knowledge bases to text, has gained much attention.

main_conclusion.value: Experimental results show that our methods achieve better precision/recall curves in sentential extraction with smoother curves in aggregated extraction which mean more stable models.

limitations.value: However, the coarse entity types are not sufficient to capture the constraints of a relation between entities.

relevance_strength.value: we propose a novel method to explore fine-grained entity type constraints

m050_external_metadata.title: Exploring Fine-grained Entity Type Constraints for Distantly Supervised Relation Extraction

title_match.candidate_title: Exploring Fine-grained Entity Type Constraints for Distantly Supervised Relation Extraction

title.value: An End-to-end Model for Entity-level Relation Extraction using Multi-instance Learning

recommendation_reason.value: We present a joint model for entity-level relation extraction from documents.

supported_research_question.value: We present a joint model for entity-level relation extraction from documents.

method.value: We present a joint model for entity-level relation extraction from documents.

data_or_scenario.value: To do so, a multi-task approach is followed that builds upon coreference resolution and gathers relevant signals via multi-instance learning with multi-level representations combining global entity and local mention information.

main_conclusion.value: We achieve state-of-theart relation extraction results on the DocRED dataset and report the first entity-level end-toend relation extraction results for future reference.

limitations.value: In contrast to other approaches -which focus on local intra-sentence mention pairs and thus require annotations on mention level -our model operates on entity level.

relevance_strength.value: We present a joint model for entity-level relation extraction from documents.

title.value: From What to Why: Improving Relation Extraction with Rationale Graph

authors_year.value: Zhenyu Zhang, Bowen Yu, Xiaobo Shu, Mengge Xue, Tingwen Liu, Li Guo (2021)

recommendation_reason.value: Which type of information affects the existing neural relation extraction (RE) models to make correct decisions is an important question.

supported_research_question.value: Which type of information affects the existing neural relation extraction (RE) models to make correct decisions is an important question.

method.value: we propose a novel RAtionale Graph (RAG) to organize such co-occurrence constraints among entity types, triggers and relations in a holistic graph view.

data_or_scenario.value: Moreover, these indicative clues are always constrained to co-occur with specific relations at the corpus level.

main_conclusion.value: By introducing two subtasks of entity type prediction and trigger labeling, we build the connection between each instance and RAG, and then leverage relevant global co-occurrence knowledge stored in the graph to improve the performance of neural RE models.

limitations.value: initially annotated the minimal contiguous indicative word span and named them trigger.

relevance_strength.value: Which type of information affects the existing neural relation extraction (RE) models to make correct decisions is an important question.
